headline: Finally, A Policy We Can All Truly Get Behind.
subtext: Experts to ensure correct biscuit-dunking protocols for maximum national benefit. Splendid.
body:
**Headline: Finally, A Policy We Can All Truly Get Behind.**

Little Puddletown Council today unveiled its landmark "Harmonious Aesthetics & Proportional Embellishment Bylaw 2024," a policy hailed by Council Spokesperson Brenda Finch as "the definitive step towards true communal visual cohesion." Designed to eradicate "neighbourly visual discord," the bylaw mandates strict adherence to a newly designated palette of 17 approved exterior paint colours, all variations of beige, charcoal, or "muted sage."

Under the comprehensive new regulations, residents must also ensure all front garden gnomes are no taller than 30cm and depict only agricultural themes, with "fantasy elements strictly prohibited to maintain gravitas." Furthermore, the bylaw stipulates that garden bird baths must be either terracotta or natural stone, and must contain water at all times for "optimal avian contemplation." Finch elaborated, "We've had too many rogue flamingos and disused bird baths. This policy ensures every visual touchpoint contributes positively to the collective psyche of Little Puddletown."

A newly formed "Neighbourly Observance Panel," comprising three retired librarians and a man known only as 'Gary from Number 47,' will conduct quarterly inspections. Non-compliance could result in fines or mandatory attendance at a "Community Visual Alignment" workshop. "This isn't about control," Finch concluded, "it's about elevating our shared existence to a plane of unblemished, beige-centric harmony."
